feed.title: Daily Digest

entry.title: USA vs. Australia live score, result, highlights from USMNT 2026 World Cup Group D match vs. Socceroos
entry.content: Following a resounding opening victory over Paraguay, the USMNT looks to secure a place in the knockout round of the 2026 FIFA World Cup as they meet Australia in their second Group D match on Friday in Seattle.

entry.title: Rays DFA former Yankees, Mets outfielder who has played for three teams in 2026
entry.content: Tampa Bay is moving on from this former Yankees and Mets veteran.

entry.title: Dolphins blockbuster trade rumor should be of interest to Cowboys, Bengals

entry.title: Horse-drawn carriage rides in New York on hold after teen tourist's death
entry.content: The union representing horse-drawn carriage drivers says it will not resume offer rides in Central Park until Tuesday in the wake of a death of a young tourist who jumped from a runaway horse carriage

entry.title: Christian Pulisic to miss US World Cup match against Australia because of calf injury
entry.content: U.S. star Christian Pulisic will miss a World Cup match against Australia because of a calf injury and was replaced in the starting lineup by Ricardo Pepi

entry.title: Why Christian Pulisic isn't playing today: What to know about injury status for USA vs. Australia
entry.content: The United States is dealing with an injury to its most recognizable player at the World Cup.

entry.title: Derrick White trade rumors give Celtics another path to improve beyond Giannis Antetokounmpo chase
entry.content: If the Celtics can't land Antetokounmpo, dealing White for another star may be their best option.

entry.title: Mariners could replace $6.25 million free agent signing at MLB trade deadline
entry.content: This free-agent signing hasn't done nearly enough to keep their job.

entry.title: Kalshi Promo Code TSNEWS: Trade $10, Get $10 World Cup Bonus for USA vs. Australia
entry.content: Sign up with the Kalshi promo code TSNEWS and trade $10 to claim a $10 bonus for USA vs. Australia on Friday.

entry.title: USA vs. Australia lineups, starting 11 for USMNT World Cup 2026 Group D game as Christian Pulisic misses out
entry.content: The United States began the 2026 FIFA World Cup in electric style, and now Mauricio Pochettino's side aim to secure a knockout stage spot and put themselves in pole position to win the group as they meet Australia on Friday.

entry.title: Mikel Brown Jr. could fall right into Dallas' lap if these eight picks go right way
entry.content: The Mavericks could draft Louisville guard Mikel Brown Jr. at No. 9 if top teams pass on him.

entry.title: Christian Pulisic injury updates: Latest news on USMNT star not playing vs. Australia in World Cup

entry.title: TV host in Argentina quits after falsely reporting that Lionel Messi’s dad died
entry.content: A TV host in Argentina resigned from her role on a popular streaming show after falsely reporting that Lionel Messi’s father had died amid his son’s run in the World Cup, an error she later blamed on unverified information fed to her through an earpiece

entry.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark conference in Ghana
entry.content: Ensuring fair compensation for those affected by legacies of enslavement and measures to address debt burdens, part of 18-point strategic roadmapA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana.Heads of state, governments and other officials adopted the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

entry.title: Norway imposes broad restrictions on AI for elementary school kids
entry.content: Norway will reportedly ban young kids from using AI in schools.

entry.title: Doom composer Bobby Prince has died
entry.content: Bobby Prince, composer for Doom and other classic shooters, has died.

entry.title: Prediction: Alphabet Stock Will Outpace the Market in 2026

entry.title: GOP senators want Chinese health monitors added to FCC's banned equipment list
entry.content: GOP Sens. Rick Scott and Dave McCormick called on the Federal Communications Commission to investigate national security risks posed by Chinese-made health wearables.

entry.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
entry.content: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5’s guardrails. Cybersecurity researchers have since signed an open letter calling the move dangerous, and Anthropic itself noted the same jailbreaks exist in other models. So is […]

entry.title: The real reason a hantavirus disaster was averted | Letters
entry.content: Dr Matthew Dryden praises an astute doctor and the value of teamwork across continents. Plus a letter from Dr Brian JonesDevi Sridhar writes about some of the global public health responses to the outbreak of hantavirus centred on the MV Hondius, but her conclusions as to how the world avoided another global outbreak failed to recognise the real reason disaster was averted (Right now, we could be living through a hantavirus disaster. The world avoided that, and this is why, 15 June).The UK Overseas Territories (UKOTs) programme funded by the Foreign Office and managed by the UK Health Security Agency supports health services in all UKOTs around the globe. These are small and vulnerable communities with very limited medical services in most cases. The key success of this lean but effective programme lies in close communication and strengthening the health services. Continue reading...

entry.title: Emergency medicine is in crisis – why is this allowed to continue? | Letters
entry.content: Dr Carole Gavin says if the government fails to act it will bear the responsibility for yet more avoidable deaths, while Sarah Brown describes her mother’s experienceThe experience of working in A&E described by Sophie (Patients are dying in A&E corridors – but I’ve seen how things could be different, 11 June) will be familiar to every member of staff working in a UK emergency department. Not only are patients dying on our corridors with no privacy or dignity due to lack of space and hospital beds, but we know that many more will die later as a consequence of their prolonged emergency department stay (More than 1,300 deaths a month in England due to long A&E waits, figures suggest, 8 June).I have been an emergency physician for more than 30 years during which time the advances in emergency medicine have been life changing, with acute interventions for once untreatable conditions such as stroke and heart attacks now routinely available. Unfortunately despite all of the advances in medical science, in England in 2026, we are now unable to provide even the most basic, humane emergency care. The government appears to be prepared to accept these deaths and when we repeatedly try to raise the alarm we are told NHS performance is improving as there are fewer patients waiting in A&E for more than four hours. However those are the well patients who will go home, while the seriously ill patients wait for up to 48 hours for admission to a bed – something that would have been unimaginable a few years ago. I am amazed on a daily basis by the resilience of the patients and staff in the face of this disaster, but fail to comprehend why this national crisis is allowed to continue. Continue reading...

entry.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
entry.content: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.

entry.title: World Cup 2026 viewer's guide: Schedule, times, channels to watch biggest stars and matches this summer

entry.title: Spain player reportedly denied entry to own World Cup camp
entry.content: Borja Iglesias was reportedly denied entry to Spain’s World Cup base in Tennessee after security staff failed to recognise the Celta Vigo striker. The Spain international was stopped outside the team camp and asked for identification before the situation was resolved.

entry.content: The Red Sox visit the Mariners.

entry.title: Canada vow to win group for Koné after suspected broken leg
entry.content: World Cup co-hosts Canada want to win Group B for Ismaël Koné after he became the latest squad member to suffer a suspected broken leg. He was having surgery in the aftermath of Thursday's 6-0 victory over Qatar in Vancouver, Canada'a first ever men's World Cup win.

entry.title: Israel-Hezbollah fighting flares up as U.S.-Iran talks delayed
entry.content: The U.S.-Iran deal faces an early test as fighting in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah reignites and next-phase talks are postponed.

entry.title: Man charged in death of girl after life sentence commuted by Kristi Noem
entry.content: McKenna Wendel was last seen alive on March 14 and her body was found outside Brookings, an hour's drive north of Sioux Falls, on March 19.

entry.title: What's missing from the Epstein files?
entry.content: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

entry.title: What's open and closed for Juneteenth 2026?
entry.content: Juneteenth will affect banking, mail service and financial markets, although retailers and restaurants are largely staying open.

entry.title: No time to rewatch 'House of the Dragon'? Here's everything to know for Season 3
entry.content: 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 is here. Here's everything you need to remember from the first two seasons of the Targaryen family war.

entry.title: 'Angry, Raucous, and Shamelessly Gorgeous' lives up to its title, but the plot lumbers
entry.content: LaTanya Richardson Jackson directs the L.A. premiere of Pearl Cleage's 'Angry, Raucous, and Shamelessly Gorgeous' at the Geffen Playhouse

entry.title: How to have the best Sunday in L.A., according to Patton Oswalt
entry.content: Comedian Patton Oswalt steers readers to Studio City's farmers market for tamales and strong coffee, then to North Hollywood's Last Bookstore and Cahuenga's cozy, cat-filled Iliad for long, screen-free reading sessions.

entry.title: As Hollywood leans into AI, the real battle is over likeness and who gets paid
entry.content: Artificial intelligence is evolving rapidly, and the entertainment industry is under pressure to keep adapting. At UCLA's annual Entertainment Symposium, Hollywood's key lawyers and dealmakers debate the future.

entry.title: Remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur flood homes and force evacuations in Gulf states
entry.content: The remnants of Tropical Storm Arthur were battering parts of the southeastern U.S. with heavy rain, sparking flash flooding.

entry.title: One Tech Tip: Watch out for scams when buying World Cup tickets
entry.content: Soccer fans should be on the watch for cyber scammers targeting those seeking last-minute World Cup tickets

entry.title: 'Sugar,' with Colin Farrell as an alien private eye, gets a new and improved second season
entry.content: Colin Farrell is back for Season 2 of Apple TV's L.A.-based neo-noir 'Sugar,' which has a new showrunner and sports a new look.

entry.title: Amy Griffin sues woman who said author stole her stories of rape for hit memoir 'The Tell'
entry.content: A woman sued Amy Griffin, alleging Griffin's bestselling memoir 'The Tell' is filled with stolen stories of rape. Now Griffin is suing her.

entry.title: Fans are fuming after World Cup tickets they bought through resale sites fall through
entry.content: The World Cup has been delivering thrills on the pitch, but fans have flooded social media with complaints about tickets that never arrived, orders canceled at the last minute and hours spent trying to sort out problems between FIFA’s ticketing system ...

entry.title: What's the HELOC and home equity loan interest rate forecast for summer 2026?
entry.content: HELOC and home equity rates have been on the move lately. Here's where those rates could head next.

entry.title: When could homeowners realistically expect to refinance? 4 lending experts weigh in
entry.content: The right time to refinance depends on a few factors, experts say, including your current mortgage loan rate.

entry.content: Caitlin Clark entered Thursday’s game against the Atlanta Dream with a lot of hype surrounding her. She debuted her first Nike signature shoe, and WNBA fans around the country tuned in to the nationally-televised matchup. The Indiana Fever superstar guard…

entry.title: Fever tie brutal WNBA record in nightmare loss to Dream
entry.content: Indiana is going to have to be better than this going forward.

entry.title: Oklahoma Sooners add speedy outfielder in the transfer portal
entry.content: The Oklahoma Sooners added a speedy outfielder to their ranks on Wednesday.

entry.content: The Malaysian is gunning to be the next winner of the US$100,000 contract this Friday.

entry.title: Yankees bullpen allows eighth-inning grand slam in 5-1 loss to White Sox
entry.content: Andrew Benintendi launched a pinch-hit grand slam off Camilo Doval in the eighth inning Thursday night and the Chicago White Sox ended a nine-game losing streak at Yankee Stadium with a 5-1 victory over New York.

entry.title: Cam Boozer’s link to the Jazz could point to their real plan for pick No. 2
entry.content: For months now, the talk surrounding the Utah Jazz and the No. 2 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft has revolved around a single question: which prospect offers the most potential? AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cam Boozer each have their own supporters and each one makes a strong case to be near the top of that year’s draft.

entry.title: MLB News: Owners propose major changes to MLB Draft
entry.content: The owners want a major change in the way amateur players sign their first professional deal.

entry.title: Team USA preparing to face Australia in World Cup match in Seattle
entry.content: After getting off to a strong start, the U.S. team is getting ready to face off against Australia in Seattle on June 19th. The two teams are tied for first in Group D, the U.S. with the goal advantage after netting four against Paraguay. Former professional soccer player Dax McCarty joins NBC News’ Tom Llamas to break down what to expect on the pitch and other happenings as the World Cup continues.

entry.title: California labor union offers to scale back billionaire tax proposal after pushback
entry.content: A California labor union is offering to scale back a proposal to temporarily increase taxes on billionaires

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
If you want to give up, remember why you started.
Lucky Numbers: 5, 91, 42, 95, 89, 27

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Pour your liquid coffee creamer into your coffee cup BEFORE you pour in the coffee. The coffee pouring stirs up the creamer, so you don't dirty a spoon!

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: No piece of square dry paper can be folded in half more than 7 times

entry.title: Quote of the Day — J.R.R. Tolkien
entry.content: “All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given to us.” — J.R.R. Tolkien

entry.title: Five years after a Navajo elder vanished, the man who robbed her was released from prison
entry.content: Five years to the day that Navajo elder Ella Mae Begay disappeared from her home in a remote corner of Arizona, the man who acknowledged beating her, stealing her truck and leaving her on the roadside is free from prison.

entry.title: With a sledgehammer and a shovel, volunteers raced to save passengers in Texas plane crash
entry.content: The business jet barreled cockeyed down the dark highway, knocking down one light pole after another, an orange glow of sparks trailing it.

entry.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
entry.content: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

entry.title: Court orders Ohio restrictions on kids' use of social media restored
entry.content: Ohio's law requiring children under 16 to get parental consent to use social media apps must be restored, a divided panel of the Sixth Circuit Court of Appeals ruled Thursday.

entry.title: Rep. Adriano Espaillat facing Mamdani-backed challenger in New York Democratic primary
entry.content: Democratic Rep. Adriano Espaillat of New York is facing a primary challenger from his left, who is endorsed by New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Political strategists Erin Maguire and Chuck Rocha join with analysis.

entry.title: Trump's goals for the Iran war and what he's saying now
entry.content: When the U.S.-Iran conflict began, President Trump laid out a litany of aggressive war aims. Here's what the president and his top aides said then — and how their views have changed.

entry.title: Trump awards Medal of Honor to 3 veterans of the Vietnam, Afghanistan wars
entry.content: In a ceremony at the White House, the president paid tribute to retired Marine Corps Maj. James Capers Jr., retired Army Maj. Nicholas Dockery and Marine Col. John W. Ripley, who died in 2008.

entry.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
entry.content: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

entry.title: UN reports record violations of children in conflict
entry.content: A new United Nations report says nearly 25,000 children caught in conflict were victims of a record number of violations last year, including killings, rape and recruitment to fight

entry.title: Police shooting of a 1-year-old Mississippi boy ignites tension between police and Black residents
entry.content: The fatal shooting of a 1-year-old boy by police who were responding to a shoplifting call this week has ignited simmering tensions between police and Black residents in the small town of Senatobia, Mississippi.

entry.title: Senator urges FAA to reject pressure from Trump to approve triumphal arch
entry.content: Democratic Sen. Tammy Duckworth has urged the Federal Aviation Administration to reject pressure from President Donald Trump to approve a 250-foot triumphal arch, citing concerns over aviation safety

entry.title: States not required to give community-based care for those with disabilities: DOJ opinion
entry.content: The Office of Legal Counsel opinion released Thursday said states aren't required by law to integrate mentally disabled patients with their peers by providing community or home-based care.

entry.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
entry.content: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

entry.title: South Dakota man whose life sentence was commuted by Noem now implicated in his niece's death
entry.content: South Dakota man whose life sentence was commuted by Noem now implicated in his niece's death

entry.title: Ebola outbreak zone cases up almost 40 percent in a week
entry.content: Ebola outbreak zone cases up almost 40 percent in a week

entry.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
entry.content: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

entry.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
entry.content: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

entry.title: Which edge prospect in the 2027 NFL Draft that would help the Vikings?

entry.title: Why Passing Is So Hard with the NASCAR Next Gen on Short Tracks
entry.content: It's no secret that the NASCAR Next Gen car has had a hard time on short tracks and road courses because it's so hard to pass. Let's dig into the details of how yaw angle and downforce impact passing on short tracks and what the latest solution from NASCAR will do.

entry.title: MLB pays tribute to former Red's 'greatest statistical season' ever
entry.content: MLB paid tribute to Gary Redus' 1978 season for the Billings Mustangs, calling it "the greatest statistical season in professional baseball history."

entry.title: 'City united after so long': New Yorkers rejoice in Knicks' championship parade
entry.content: The New York Knicks won the NBA Finals on Saturday, generating a citywide energy that culminated in a massive parade on Thursday.

entry.title: Watch Live: Obama Presidential Center opening ceremony
entry.content: The Obama Presidential Center, museum and library opens in Chicago with a star-studded grand opening ceremony and public watch party on Midway Plaisance.

entry.title: World Cup group scenarios 2026: How teams can qualify and advance to Round of 32 knockout bracket
entry.content: As the World Cup group stages enters its second round of matches, some nations will be booking their places in the knockout stages, while others will be heading home.
